---
title: "Winsorization Comparison Figures"
author: "Felicity Simpson"
date: "2026-02-26"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)

```

```{r}
# Load data
path <- "C:/Users/c3371138/Dropbox/optical-imaging/dat/AF_optical_2.csv"
dat <- read.csv(path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Optional: if you want the analytic sample only (uncomment if needed)
# dat <- dat %>% filter(record_id >= 2001)

# Variables used in the winsorization figure
cog_vars <- c(
  "ComplexChoiceLatency",
  "IES_congruencyCost",
  "IES_mixCost",
  "IES_MTTICMD",
  "IES_MTTMTCMD",
  "SimpleChoiceLatency",
  "VRMDRTC",
  "VRMFRDS",
  "VRMIRTC"
)

# Fail fast if any are missing
missing_vars <- setdiff(cog_vars, names(dat))
if (length(missing_vars) > 0) {
  stop(
    paste0(
      "These variables are missing from the dataset: ",
      paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", ")
    )
  )
}

# Keep only the variables needed for plotting
dat_plot <- dat %>% dplyr::select(all_of(cog_vars))
```


```{r}
# Cleaner labels for facet titles
var_labels <- c(
  ComplexChoiceLatency = "Complex choice\nlatency",
  SimpleChoiceLatency  = "Simple choice\nlatency",
  IES_congruencyCost   = "TSWT congruency\ncost (IES)",
  IES_mixCost          = "TSWT multitasking\ncost (IES)",
  IES_MTTICMD          = "MTT incongruent\ncost (IES)",
  IES_MTTMTCMD         = "MTT multitasking\ncost (IES)",
  VRMDRTC              = "VRM delayed\nrecognition RT",
  VRMFRDS              = "VRM free recall\n(delayed)",
  VRMIRTC              = "VRM immediate\nrecognition RT"
)
```


```{r}
winsorize_sd <- function(x, z = 3.29) {
  if (!is.numeric(x)) return(x)
  if (all(is.na(x))) return(x)

  mu <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  s  <- sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)

  if (is.na(s) || s == 0) return(x)

  lower <- mu - z * s
  upper <- mu + z * s

  x_w <- x
  x_w[x_w < lower] <- lower
  x_w[x_w > upper] <- upper
  x_w
}

dat_wins <- dat_plot %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), winsorize_sd))
```


```{r}

# Long-format data: before vs after winsorization --------------------------------
long_before <- dat_plot %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = "Before Winsorization")

long_after <- dat_wins %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = "After Winsorization")

# Combine and format plotting variables -----------------------------------------
plot_long <- dplyr::bind_rows(long_before, long_after) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Full labels for legend (ordered Before then After)
    Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Before Winsorization", "After Winsorization")),

    # Short labels for panel x-axis text
    Type_short = factor(
      Type,
      levels = c("Before Winsorization", "After Winsorization"),
      labels = c("Before", "After")
    ),

    # Add spaced x positions so the two boxplots are visually separated
    x_pos = dplyr::case_when(
      Type == "Before Winsorization" ~ 1,
      Type == "After Winsorization"  ~ 3
    ),

    # Keep facet order stable
    variable = factor(variable, levels = cog_vars),

    # Cleaner facet labels
    variable_clean = dplyr::recode(as.character(variable), !!!var_labels),
    variable_clean = factor(variable_clean, levels = unname(var_labels[cog_vars]))
  )

# Boxplot figure ----------------------------------------------------------------
p_box <- ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = x_pos, y = value, fill = Type, group = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    outlier.size = 1.2,
    alpha = 0.85,
    width = 0.5,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable_clean, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Distributions Before & After Winsorization",
    x = "Winsorization Status",
    y = "Value"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(1, 3),
    labels = c("Before", "After")
  ) +
  scale_fill_discrete(
    name = "Type",
    labels = c("Before Winsorization", "After Winsorization")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9, lineheight = 0.95),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.spacing = grid::unit(0.8, "lines"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(8, 8, 12, 8)
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 1))

p_box

```

```{r}

p_hist <- ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = value, fill = Type)) +
  geom_histogram(
    position = "identity",
    alpha = 0.6,
    bins = 20,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable_clean, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    x = "Value",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  scale_fill_discrete(
    name = "Type",
    labels = c("Before Winsorization", "After Winsorization")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9, lineheight = 0.95),
    panel.spacing = grid::unit(0.8, "lines"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(8, 8, 8, 8)
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 1))

p_hist
```
